response:
To compute the **sample standard deviation** of the data set:

$$
\{10, 18, 15, -5, -14, -21\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{10 + 18 + 15 + (-5) + (-14) + (-21)}{6}
$$

$$
= \frac{10 + 18 + 15 - 5 - 14 - 21}{6}
$$

$$
= \frac{38}{6} = \frac{19}{3} \approx 6.3333
$$

---

### Step 2: Subtract the mean from each data point and square the result

$$
\begin{align*}
(10 - \frac{19}{3})^2 &= \left(\frac{30 - 19}{3}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{11}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{121}{9} \\
(18 - \frac{19}{3})^2 &= \left(\frac{54 - 19}{3}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{35}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{1225}{9} \\
(15 - \frac{19}{3})^2 &= \left(\frac{45 - 19}{3}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{26}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{676}{9} \\
(-5 - \frac{19}{3})^2 &= \left(\frac{-15 - 19}{3}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{-34}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{1156}{9} \\
(-14 - \frac{19}{3})^2 &= \left(\frac{-42 - 19}{3}\right)^2 = \left(\frac{-61}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{3721}{9} \\
